おはようございます。週末を迎える金曜日の朝、いかがお過ごしですか？  慌ただしく一週間を締めくくるタイムラインですが、今朝までにシリコンバレーから開示された巨額の資金調達、そして実務に深く入り込んだAI環境の致命的な脆弱性から、これまでの「AI＝ただの便利なツール」という認識を冷徹にアップデートすべき決定的な事実が確定しました。  ・@AnthropicAI：📈【評価額9,650億ドル（約145兆円）、10月上場に向けIPO主幹事団を確定】複数の米国報道により、Anthropicが2026年10月のNasdaq上場を目標に、Goldman Sachs・JPMorgan・Morgan Stanleyを主幹事証券として選定したことが判明。6月1日にSECへ機密S-1を提出済みで、年間経常収益（ARR）は5月時点で約470億ドルに到達、100万ドル超の契約を結ぶ大口顧客数はわずか2カ月弱で倍増しています。ライバルのOpenAIが「評価額1兆ドル未満での上場は受け入れない」というアルトマンCEOの方針から上場を2027年へ延期する報道が6月26日に出たことで、Anthropicが先に上場して業界の評価基準を定める「ファーストムーバー」の地位を握る構図が固まりつつあります。6月19日にはトランプ大統領が「Anthropicはもはや国家安全保障上の脅威とは見なしていない」とAxiosに述べ、政治的な障壁も取り除かれました。  ・@OpenAI：🛡️【AIがAIを攻撃する自動赤チーム「GPT-Red」を公開、GPT-5.6 Sol の防御力を強化】7月15日、エージェント環境がサードパーティのデータやコードを自律処理する際の「プロンプトインジェクション攻撃」に対抗するため、自己対戦型の強化学習で訓練した攻撃モデル「GPT-Red」を公開しました。内部評価では人間のレッドチームの成功率わずか13%に対し、GPT-Redは84%の確率で攻撃を成功させ、新種の「偽装された思考連鎖（fake chain-of-thought）」という攻撃パターンも新たに発見。この知見をGPT-5.6の敵対的訓練に還元する構造です。Andon Labs製のAI自動販売機を狙った実証実験では、最安値への価格改ざん・不正注文・他顧客の注文キャンセルという3つの悪意ある目標すべてを達成しました。悪用防止のためGPT-Red自体は内部専用ツールとして厳重に管理されています。  ・@cursor：⚠️【すでに修正済みの重大脆弱性「DuneSlide」に続き、拡張機能の認証情報流出リスクも新たに発覚】多くの実務現場に導入されているCursorにおいて、外部の汚染されたコンテンツ（悪意あるウェブ検索結果やMCPサーバーの応答など）を読み込むだけでサンドボックスを脱出しOSレベルで任意のコードが実行されるゼロデイ級の脆弱性「DuneSlide」（CVE-2026-50548/50549、CVSS9.8）が6月に技術詳細を公表されました。脆弱性自体は2月の責任ある開示を受けCursor 3.0（4月2日リリース）ですでに修正済みです。さらに6月29日には、インストールされた拡張機能がローカルのSQLiteデータベースに保存されたAPIキーやセッショントークンへアクセスできてしまう別の未修正の脆弱性がLayerXから報告されました。OpenAI・Anthropic・Google等の連携サービスの認証情報が悪意ある拡張機能によって窃取されるリスクがあり、報告時点では未対応のままです。外部の共有コードやリポジトリ、拡張機能を安易に作業環境へ読み込ませる運用の「死角」が改めて浮き彫りになりました。  ・@OpenRouter：⚙️【個別環境の脆弱性やベンダーのルール変更に対抗する『知能の保険』】Cursorなど特定の環境に依存しすぎず、また個別モデルの規約や突然の価格変動リスクを分散するため、OpenRouterの専用エンドポイント（MCPサーバー等）を介して、状況に応じて自動で他社モデル（DeepSeekやQwen等）へ切り替えるマルチプロバイダ運用の実需が最高潮に達しています。

Here are 7 FREE cybersecurity courses you've never heard of that you start this weekend 👩🏻‍💻👀  Everyone shares the same handful of platforms, so I went looking for the underrated ones. These are all free to start, hands-on, and beginner friendly:  1. 🔒 Root Me: root-me.org/?lang=en Hundreds of free challenges across web, network, forensics, and crypto.   2. 🛡️ AttackIQ Academy: academy.attackiq.com/ Free courses on MITRE ATT&CK, detection engineering, and purple teaming.  3. 🔍 CyberDefenders BlueYard: cyberdefenders.org/blueteam-… Free DFIR, threat hunting, and malware analysis labs that run right in your browser.  4. 🕵🏻‍♀️ Blue Team Labs Online: blueteamlabs.online/ A gamified platform covering incident response and threat hunting.   5. 🎓 BreachLab: breachlab.org/ Comprehensive offensive security training from Linux basics to red team operations.  6. 💥 Metasploit Unleashed: offsec.com/metasploit-unleas… The free ethical hacking course from OffSec, and one of the best intros to exploitation.  7. 🧪 SEED Labs: seedsecuritylabs.org/ Free university grade security labs on buffer overflows, SQL injection, and cryptography.  There's a LOT of cybersecurity training out there, so hopefully this list gives you something new to start with   Drop any other underrated training in the comments, I would love to see what the community is using!  🌟 Repost this to share with your network 🌟  #cybersecurity #cybersecuritytraining #cybersecurityjobs

AIを「安全なモデル」に仕上げるだけで十分なのでしょうか。  Google DeepMindが示したのは、将来、企業内部で使われるAIが望ましくない目標を持ち、監視を欺こうとする可能性まで想定した、「サイバーセキュリティ型のAI制御ロードマップ」です。  本稿は、現在のAIが実際に企業へ反旗を翻していると報告するものではありません。アラインメントという第一の防衛線が破れた最悪の場合を想定し、AIを潜在的な内部脅威として扱います。  既存のMITRE ATT&CKを拡張した「TRAIT&R」により、コードの改変、情報流出、研究妨害、複数のAIによる分散型攻撃などを整理しています。  そのうえで、AIの「監視回避能力」と「攻撃実行能力」に応じて、思考・行動の監視、隔離、権限管理、リアルタイムの遮断、緊急停止など15の対策を段階的に強化する枠組みを提案しています。  重要なのは、AIの目標を正しく保つことだけに安全性を委ねず、たとえ望ましくない目標を持っていても、外側のシステムで行動を検出し、阻止し、被害から復旧する「第二の防衛線」を、フロンティアAIラボが公式のロードマップとして打ち出した点です。  ただし、これは実証済みの万能策ではなく、今後の実装と検証を必要とする設計図です。  GDM AI Control Roadmap. Mary Phuong, Erik Jenner, Laurent Simon, Lewis Ho, Rohin Shah, Sebastian Farquhar, Scott Coull. Arxiv:2607.13087

Build projects that prove you can do the job.  Top 3 projects to build:  1. SOC investigation with written incident report  Investigate real attacks on a VM, analyze logs, and write a report: timeline, IOCs, MITRE ATT&CK mapping, recommendations. The report is the product.  2. Third-party vendor risk assessment  Onboard a fictional SaaS vendor. Send a security questionnaire, review a SOC 2, score the risks, and write a risk acceptance memo with remediation asks.  Assessment workbook + 1-page exec summary.  3. Identity lifecycle (JML) audit  Set up Entra ID or Okta, simulate joiner/mover/leaver scenarios, audit for orphaned accounts, excessive permissions, and missing offboarding. Document gaps against a framework. Shows you understand access risk, not just tools.  Build these. Document them. Talk about them.

7️⃣ ### The Tester Critic  The Tester audits relevance and correctness of mitigation.  Every MITRE technique ID mentioned in the Architect’s output is resolved against the authoritative ATT&CK database before the Tester runs.  This matters: in early testing, an Architect model assigned wrong names to T1590.005 and T1565.001. The Tester, not recognising the labels, flagged them as non-existent technique IDs. The synthesis step then generated a false contradiction. The fix was grounding: resolve every T####.### ID against the ground truth database established, inject the verified names, and the Tester now argues from the same authoritative reference.

4️⃣ ## Layer 2 — The ARC Framework and MITRE ATLAS  For architectures involving AI or ML components, a second detection layer runs on top of RAPIDS.  The ARC Framework (Agentic Risk Capability Framework) is published by Singapore’s GovTech under their responsible AI initiative. It defines nine risk categories for agentic AI systems — Integrity, Safety, Security, Privacy, Transparency, Accountability, Fairness, Resilience, and Societal — each with per-component scoring rules and control benchmarks.  ThreatAssessor loads the full ARC risk and controls catalog and applies it when the architecture contains AI/ML components like LLM APIs, vector databases, or embedding services.  MITRE ATLAS (Adversarial Threat Landscape for Artificial-Intelligence Systems) provides the technique taxonomy for AI-specific attack patterns — prompt injection (AML.T0051), model extraction, training data poisoning.  ARC controls are annotated with ATLAS technique IDs, giving AI threats the same authoritative citation structure as traditional MITRE ATT&CK findings.

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 مجموعة RipperSec الهندية تنشر ادعاءً بالوصول إلى نظام تحكم صناعي (OT) تزعم أنه تابع لشركة Türk Telekom في تركيا 🇹🇷  القناة المرتبطة بالمجموعة نشرت مقطعًا يُظهر واجهة تحكم HMI باللغة التركية لنظام تكييف/تحكم بيئي.  🔹 بحسب ما نشرته القناة: • الادعاء بالوصول إلى نظام OT تابع لـ Türk Telekom • الواجهة تُظهر قيم تحكم بيئي: درجة الحرارة (SICAKLIK) والرطوبة (NEM) بقيم SET وGERÇEK (فعلية)، ودرجة الحرارة الخارجية، ومراوح في وضع التشغيل الآلي (OTOMATİK)  🔎 وحتى على مستوى الوصول نفسه: عرض لقطة HMI يثبت رؤية للواجهة لا سيطرة تشغيلية عليها. اللقطة تُظهر قيم مراقبة في الوضع الآلي، دون تنفيذ أي أمر فعلي. لا يوجد حتى الآن أي تأكيد مستقل على صحة الادعاء أو ارتباطه بـ Türk Telekom.

Armenian Petroglyphs from 9,000-3,000 BC  Cave Writing from Lake Sevan. Before hieroglyphic writing was developed between 900 and 700 BC, the inhabitants of the Armenian Highlands expressed themselves by carving and painting on the rocks.  These three pictograms were completed between the 8th and 4th millennia BC (from 9,000 to 3,000 BC). According to archaeologists, the drawings are associated with Neolithic cultures, especially in the higher mountain regions, including Aragats, Armaghan, and the Lake Sevan basin.  Armenian language and its roots  The Indo-European language group is one of the most widely spoken language families in the world. Around 3000 BC, the proto-Indo-European languages were probably closely connected to each other. But by 2000 BC, Greek and two extinct languages, Hittite and Sanskrit, already differed one from another quite significantly.  Although changes in grammar and definitions have taken place, vocabulary analyses show that people of ancient Indo-European culture were farmers, knew how to handle iron, and had developed cattle breeding.  Recent discoveries also reveal common ties with the Kurgan people, who lived in the steppes to the west of the Urals.  Samples of Armenian hieroglyphs were revealed in excavations in Karmir Blur, where ceramics, bronze cups, and cylindrical shells were unearthed. This type of writing was used by the people living in the Ararat valley long before the establishment of the Kingdom of Van.  Famous English poet Lord Byron studied the Armenian language on the island of St. Lazarus in the monastery of the Mekhitarists in Venice, Italy, from 1816 to 1823. He wrote: “It is a rich language, however, and would amply repay any one the trouble of learning it”. In a note of April 11, 1818, he wrote: “You will not neglect my Armenians.”  By PeopleOfAr Source: University of Minnesota

Breaking News: Self-styled Ambazonia separatist commander "General Sagat," identified as Tandu Lazarus, was killed this Thursday, July 16, during a military operation in Ekona, South West region, Cameroon News Agency reported, citing military sources.  Sagat, who led a group known as the Fako Mountain Lions, was reportedly killed after troops acting on intelligence about the movement of suspected separatist fighters carried out an operation in the area.   The Fako Mountain Lions is a separatist militia active in the Fako Division and is considered loyal to Samuel Ikome Sako, a diaspora-based separatist leader who heads a faction of the Ambazonia Interim Government.  Troops reportedly intercepted two men travelling on a motorcycle before carrying out an ambush. General Sagat was killed during the operation, while a second suspected fighter was injured and arrested.  The military said it recovered two mobile phones and an undisclosed amount of cash from them.   #Cameroon #NorthWest #SouthWest #Ekona #Ambazonia #Conflict #MMINews

🔴 Morena mantiene amplia ventaja rumbo a la gubernatura de Campeche: encuesta.  A menos de un año del arranque del proceso electoral de 2027, Morena se perfila como el partido favorito para conservar la gubernatura de Campeche, con una ventaja de casi dos a uno sobre su competidor más cercano, de acuerdo con una encuesta de QM Estudios de Opinión y Heraldo Media Group.  El estudio señala que, si hoy fueran las elecciones, 47% de los encuestados votaría por Morena, mientras que Movimiento Ciudadano obtendría 18% de las preferencias. En los escenarios de posibles candidaturas, Pablo Gutiérrez Lazarus encabeza las preferencias internas de Morena con 41%, seguido por Carlos Ucán Yam con 16%.  #Campeche #Morena #Elecciones2027 #Gubernatura #PabloGutiérrezLazarus #Encuesta #Política #TomásZapataLive

July 15, 2026 jesusandscience.org/2026/07/…  Dear Friends,  This week, five men prayed with me according to the pattern of Luke 18:13 & Romans 10:9.  A young man, mid 20s, in the UK, wrote,  “Hi James! I struggle to be convinced by Jesus’ resurrection, could we organise a meeting or could you help me in any way?”  He has an education in movie production, but since COVID, that field has cratered. So he is presently working in a primary school. A very friendly young man who listened carefully and readily accepted the word of truth. He is being mentored by his associate now using “Growing in Christ”.  Acts 16:14 “One of those listening was a woman from the city of Thyatira named Lydia, a dealer in purple cloth. She was a worshiper of God, and the Lord opened her heart to respond to Paul’s message.” On the advice of wise counsel, over the next few weeks, I will be winding down most of the one-on-one appointments I had scheduled with seekers. I was getting overwhelmed by too many one-on-one appointments. Though I never mention it, in many of those scheduled meeting times, the seeker doesn’t show up at our agreed time, and I wait for up to 15 minutes. So it can really slice up an evening. I have declined many other activities over the years in order to attend these one-on-one meetings, so when they do not show up, it is troubling. I truly enjoy speaking one-on-one to share the gospel. It is my greatest joy in life. But I am getting overwhelmed by the level of interest.  Brandon set up a Webinar-based system, and I will hold two sessions per week, during which I can meet with several people at a time. It is not as personal as the one-on-one, but it is the only way to ensure my physical survival. This way, I can meet with people in small groups of 3 to 7, while the technology itself would permit meetings of hundreds. Also, if a person or two do not show up, the meeting can still ensue. So far, the meetings are held twice per week: on Saturdays at noon and again at 8 pm, so people can access them easily from anywhere in the world without work or sleep-time conflicts. We have held two such sessions, and while there are bugs to work out and refinements that we will make over the next few weeks, it is a far more efficient course. Additionally, others have signed up to serve as mentors, which was desperately needed.  The people below attended the webinar.  A Canadian videographer in his 40s wrote, in part:  “Hi Dr. Tour, Thank you for making time to meet with people who are genuinely searching. I’ve been wrestling with the physical resurrection of Jesus for quite a while. I’m hoping to better understand why you believe the resurrection is the best explanation and to ask a few honest questions if time allows.”  After the webinar, he wrote,  “Thanks so much for putting this together. I just finished your Zoom lecture today, and I really appreciate you taking the time to share your insight. I’ll be straight with you—I’ve been looking for a solid male mentor for over four years now. Hearing you talk about this program as a genuine labour of love really resonated with me. I’m genuinely motivated to learn and grow, and I’m excited about the possibility of working with someone experienced who can help guide me forward, both personally and spiritually. Thanks again to you and everyone who made this happen. Looking forward to connecting.”  Rice Broocks will assign the mentor for this Canadian seeker.  Hebrews 10:25 “not forsaking our own assembling together, as is the habit of some, but encouraging one another; and all the more as you see the day drawing near.”  Next was a man from Romania with many questions. He wrote, in part:  “Why is it that Jesus’ corpse was not simply revived, like Lazarus? Why the challenge of delayed recognition? Mary Magdalene thinks he is the gardener until he speaks her name: Mary (John 20:14 16). The two disciples on the road to Emmaus walk with him for a long time, but their eyes were kept from recognizing him until the breaking of bread (Luke 24:16, 30 31). The disciples by the Sea of Tiberias do not immediately know it is Jesus on the shore (John 21:4). I do have a hard time processing these evidences. Kind regards, _______.”  We met on the webinar. Again, it suffered from a lack of one-on-one interactions, during which I could not see his face, though he could see my face. After I presented the gospel, he acknowledged that he had prayed with me. He also asked this same question during the question-and-answer time. I cited the psychological study titled “Recognizing Familiar Faces Out of Context” (doi.org/10.1177/030100662098… ). Where unexpected faces are often not recognized, as would be Jesus after rising from the dead. And I mentioned other possible reasons: disfigurement from the scourging and crucifixion, as we know he still bore the wounds on his hands and side, and possibly the Lord needed to open the eyes of their hearts to recognize him.   Rice Broocks will assign the mentor for this Romanian seeker through one of his churches in Romania.  The next was a man who wrote,   “Hello, my friend sent me this video youtube.com/shorts/4n0LFx63A… As a favor to him I am emailing you. Not fully sure what the challenge is all about. Look forward to hearing from you.”  After the webinar he wrote,   “I prayed along with you. and I will think about this for a while and chew on it. I don’t have any questions yet but will when with mentor.”  Chuck Lloyd will assign the mentor.  The last is a man in Nigeria who wrote, before the webinar:  “Why should I believe in the resurrection of Jesus?”  Post webinar, he indicated that he prayed along with me, and he wrote:  “the mentorship and training would go a long way sir.”  Rice Broocks will assign the mentor for this Nigerian seeker through one of his churches in Nigeria.  Like always, I taught them how to read slowly, prayerfully, and pensively, staring in the Gospel of John. Every morning for 15 minutes. Every morning, not just 3 days per week. We also followed that up with these same instructions in writing sent to each man. Mentors are assigned to all to get them started in one-on-one Bible studies.  Understandably, the acceptance rate for the webinar system is not yet as high as that for one-on-one meetings. So we need to refine the webinar format and strike a balance among several factors for the group sessions: privacy, the ability for them to speak rather than just type questions, the need to protect the identities of those logging in from persecuting countries, and my ability to reach the many that seek to hear from me about the message of Jesus without my getting wiped out (I have a few other day-jobs). So, as Brandon learns the capabilities of the commercial webinar system, may the Lord provide wisdom so we know how best to move forward. Please pray that the Lord provides wisdom and an anointing on those webinar times so that many will be saved.   Lord, I pray, give us the creativity of Bezalel in using the technology, and the passion of Paul for the lost.  While the gospel message will never change! Sinners are saved through the death of the Son of God and his resurrection from the dead. Glorious Jesus!  Romans 10:1 “Brethren, my heart’s desire and my prayer to God for them is for their salvation.”  God bless, Dr. James Tour

„Beast Machine” infrastructure: control is achieved through  “no man might buy or sell”   WARNING   The practical risk case:  crypto/wallets vs. bank deposits No insurance backstop.  Bank deposits are covered by government deposit insurance (FDIC in the US) up to a set limit, but crypto assets — even when held “through” an FDIC-insured bank — get zero protection if the exchange or platform fails (FDIC Advisory via Foodman CPAs, Money).   If your bank fails, you get your money back. If your exchange or wallet provider fails, you may get nothing. Irreversibility is absolute. A fraudulent bank charge can usually be disputed and reversed. A blockchain transaction cannot — send funds to the wrong address, fall for a phishing link, or get hacked, and there is no “reset password” or recovery line (Finance Monthly, Motley Fool).  The custody trap cuts both ways. If you self-custody (hold your own private keys), you own your assets outright — but lose the seed phrase or private key and the funds are gone forever, with no support desk able to help (~20% of all Bitcoin is estimated permanently lost this way) (Omar Moonis — Self-Custody Risk, [Chainalysis via Omar Moonis]). If you use a custodial exchange instead, you’re exposed to that platform’s solvency and security — Mt. Gox, FTX, and the 2025 Bybit hack ($1.46 billion, the largest crypto theft on record) show how catastrophically that can fail (TRM Labs 2026 Crypto Crime Report).  Attack surface is large and growing. In 2025 alone, roughly $2.87 billion was stolen across ~150 hacks; the first months of 2026 already exceed $1 billion lost to state-sponsored hackers (North Korea’s Lazarus Group in particular), AI-driven scams, and smart-contract exploits (TRM Labs, Ledger 2026, 24/7 Wall Street).  A future-facing threat banks don’t face the same way. Quantum computing is considered an “existential threat” to crypto’s cryptography specifically — researchers estimate 35–50% of circulating Bitcoin could be exposed to a quantum attack on public keys, since balances and keys are permanently visible on-chain. Bank ledgers aren’t exposed this way because they aren’t public.[reuters]  No regulator is standing behind you. Banking is a heavily regulated system designed explicitly to reduce insolvency risk and protect depositors; crypto has comparatively thin regulatory cover, meaning “you are on your own” if something goes wrong (Investor.gov Bulletin, Foodman CPAs).  In short: a bank deposit trades some freedom/privacy for institutional insurance, reversibility, and regulatory backstops. A crypto wallet trades those protections for autonomy — and that autonomy comes with unforgiving, irreversible consequences for any single mistake, hack, or platform failure  <<<<>>>>  My dots - Ones you take your cash out of your bank account nobody bother how you spend it.  Your privacy has total anonymity.  Digital cash will be noted exactly - every single movement - you will be recorded in your data sheet - every your breath you will take .   You will think that AI bot is anonymous but it is bs it is the most detailed surveillance possible   And now do your research on running your bank account on platform - it will anyway run through banks   But it is the magic of bots - and how you will be „managed“ or „mismanaged“   You know you are in Matrix.

Fedorov just dropped the receipts before stepping down and it is worth dissecting without the usual victory lap fluff. From 2019 onward he built the bridge between civilian tech momentum and actual battlefield impact. No poetry about unbreakable spirit, just numbers, names, and the structural headaches that still choke results. He called out Trukha directly for the attacks, noted the new Russian-citizen owner of her outlet hunting ways back into drone supply chains for the Ukrainian military, and laid out how digital registries exposed entire brigades where up to half the personnel either went AWOL or filed transfer requests. The servicemen registry is already 95 percent populated. That is not abstract reform, that is forensic accountability finally catching up to Soviet-era personnel black holes.  UNITED24 funded the Army of Drones push, bought serious batches of sea drones that started hitting the Russian fleet, stood up the first strike drone companies and then the Unmanned Systems Forces. The procurement model is blunt: 80 percent of drones bought from the ten most effective producers per category, 20 percent through open tenders that new players can actually enter. One early shell tender cut the 155mm unit price by a thousand dollars and saved a hundred million overall. The current 150,000-drone tender has 59 companies competing with expected 30 percent price compression. Private firms can now test interceptors inside live air defense loops and get paid per confirmed Shahed kill. Some units are already knocking down 90 percent. Add eBals, Brave1 Market as the Amazon for war, performance-based purchases where confirmed strikes earn units more drones, and a billion hryvnias disbursed to the sharpest outfits: Magyar, Alpha, Lazarus group, Achilles, K2. Ground robotic complexes and explosives markets opened too. Twelve thousand NRCs bought in 2025, fifty thousand planned for 2026. Starlink whitelists negotiated with Musk proved a war-turning factor that denied Russians easy drone command links. No full replacement found. Small air defense layers now targeting 95 percent drone kills plus some cruise missiles.  The doctrinal shift Fedorov described is the part Western analysts still half-digest. Technology first destroys enemy positions, then infantry moves. Lose metal, not people. Drone-assault units like Code 9.2 and Khartia retook Kupiansk on that model. Operation Ashan saw 700 Russian armor pieces destroyed at depths up to 50 kilometers; from May to October 2025 Moscow could not mount major mechanized assaults. Ashan-2 took out 250 artillery systems in two nights. The overarching frame is Air-Land-Economy: intercept incoming missiles and drones, halt ground advances, degrade the financial machinery that sustains the war. Territorial losses shrank in 2026 while liberated ground grew. Russian assault tactics degraded from mass mechanized pushes and large infantry groups to small teams or lone operators. That is not narrative, that is measured degradation visible on the sensor logs.  The problems he admitted matter more than the wins for anyone serious about outcomes. Drones, shells, and resources still get handed out manually; loyal commanders eat first. Fedorov openly disagreed with Syrskyi, said Zelenskyy had no plan to replace the commander-in-chief, and that he learned to coexist. After that the General Staff started blocking every proposed reform, personnel move, and project. Fedorov credited Syrskyi for 2022 but stressed the war has mutated while the command culture stayed frozen in authoritarian rigidity. Ukrainian soldiers can and will take responsibility when given resources, clear objectives, and competent leadership. TCK structures report to Ground Forces which report to the commander-in-chief and General Staff. Patching one link solves nothing; the whole chain needs redesign.  Bottom line from the outgoing minister: Ukraine has engineered the technological toolkit that can stop Moscow while shedding fewer lives.

SAVE THIS POST.  101 QUESTIONS EVERY CHRISTIAN PARENT SHOULD ASK THEIR CHILD BEFORE THEY GROW UP  One day, someone will ask your child a question you are not in the room to answer.  Why do you believe in God?  Has science not made God unnecessary?  If God is good, why is there evil?  Why should the Bible decide what is true?  Why forgive someone who is not sorry?  Why love an enemy who hates you?  Why did Jesus have to die?  Are you afraid of death?  What is your life actually for?  And one day, the person asking these questions may not be a teacher, an atheist, a friend, or a stranger.  It may be life itself.  A death.  A betrayal.  An unanswered prayer.  A scientific discovery.  A political crisis.  A moral temptation.  A painful failure.  Or unexpected success.  And in that moment...  Mummy and Daddy may not be in the room.  Perhaps we know our children's shoe sizes, favourite foods, school subjects, and career dreams...  but have never really discovered the world being built inside their minds.  So talk to your children.  Not only preach at them.  Not only take them to church.  Ask them questions.  And when their answers frighten you, don't immediately silence them.  If your child says, “Loving your enemies sounds foolish,” ask why.  If they say, “I don't understand why God allowed this,” listen.  If they say, “Sometimes I'm afraid of dying,” don't shame them.  If they say, “I'm not sure I believe that,” resist the temptation to panic.  Truth is not so fragile that it must be protected from the sincere questions of a child.  Here are 101 conversations I believe Christian parents should have with their children before they grow up.  Not as an examination.  Not to manufacture perfect “Christian answers.”  But to discover what they truly think, what they secretly fear, what they have merely memorized, and what they genuinely believe.  HERE'S THE LIST:  1. Who is God to you, not just what have you been taught about Him?  2. If someone asked why you believe God exists, what would you say?  3. What do you think it truly means to know God?  4. What does loving God with all your heart, soul, and mind mean to you?  5. Is there anything about God you find difficult to understand or accept?  6. If you could ask God one completely honest question, what would it be?  7. What do you understand by the Gospel?  8. If a friend asked you to explain the Gospel without using “church words,” what would you say?  9. What do you understand by salvation, and why do you think it is necessary?  10. In your opinion, what is humanity's greatest problem?  11. Can education, money, technology, or good government completely solve the deepest problem of the human heart? Why?  12. Why do you think Jesus came into the world?  13. Why did Jesus have to die?  14. What does the resurrection of Jesus mean to you?  15. What is the difference between being a good person and being saved?  16. What do grace and repentance mean to you?  17. Can someone know many Bible verses, attend church, and still not truly know Christ?  18. How would you know whether Christianity is truly your faith and not simply your parents' religion?  19. Why do you personally believe in Jesus?  20. Is there anything about Christianity you secretly struggle to believe?  21. Do you believe the Bible should be the final arbiter of truth for a Christian? Why?  22. What should you do when your feelings tell you one thing and Scripture appears to say another?  23. Can something feel right and still be wrong?  24. Does something become true because most people believe it?  25. How can we distinguish between “the Bible says this” and “this is how I have interpreted the Bible”?  26. What should you do when sincere Christians disagree about a Bible passage?  27. If Scripture showed that something you strongly believed was wrong, would you be willing to change your mind?  28. Is asking difficult questions about God or the Bible a sign of unbelief, or can honest questions sometimes lead us deeper into truth?  29. Do you believe God created the universe? Why?  30. When you look at the stars, planets, oceans, forests, animals, and biodiversity, what do they make you think about?  31. Do you think life and the universe are ultimately products of mere chance, or do you believe there is purpose behind existence? Why?  32. What do you understand about the Big Bang and evolution?  33. Do you think science and belief in God must be enemies?  34. If science explains how a natural process happens, does that automatically mean God has nothing to do with it?  35. What kinds of questions can science answer very well, and what kinds of questions may require philosophy or theology?  36. Can a person be a serious scientist and a sincere Christian?  37. If scientific evidence appears to conflict with the way you understand a Bible passage, what should you do?  38. Do you think studying nature can help us appreciate God's creation more deeply?  39. If human beings are made in the image of God, how should that change the way we treat every human life?  40. If God is Creator, what responsibility do we have toward animals, forests, oceans, and the earth?  41. If God is good, why do you think evil exists?  42. Who or what do you believe is responsible for evil?  43. Should we blame Satan for every evil choice human beings willingly make?  44. Why do people sometimes knowingly choose things that hurt other people?  45. Why do you think innocent people sometimes suffer?  46. What would you say to someone who asks, “Where was God when this terrible thing happened?”  47. Can God bring good out of evil without calling the evil itself good?  48. Do you think we will understand every painful thing that happens to us in this life?  49. How should a Christian respond when God seems silent?  50. How do we resist evil without allowing hatred to turn us into the very thing we oppose?  51. What does it mean to overcome evil with good?  52. What is the difference between justice and revenge?  53. Can you fight injustice without hating or dehumanizing the person doing wrong?  54. Why did Jesus command us to love our enemies?  55. Does loving your enemies sometimes sound foolish or unfair to you? Why?  56. Why should we forgive people who may not deserve our forgiveness?  57. Does forgiveness mean pretending that nothing happened?  58. Can you forgive someone and still establish wise boundaries?  59. Have you ever tried to sincerely pray for someone who hurt you? What was it like?  60. How does the fact that Christ loved us while we were sinners change the way we look at people who wrong us?  61. Which requires more strength: taking revenge or refusing to let hatred control your heart?  62. What do you think death is?  63. Are you afraid of dying? Why or why not?  64. How do you think a Christian should view death, with fear, faith, sorrow, hope, or boldness?  65. Can a Christian grieve deeply and still have faith?  66. Why did Jesus weep at Lazarus' tomb even though He knew Lazarus would rise?  67. What do you think happens after death?  68. What does the resurrection of Christ teach us about the grave?  69. Why does the Bible sometimes describe death as an enemy, yet Christians can face it with hope?  70. If you knew you had only one year left to live, what would suddenly become important to you?  71. What do you think makes a life well lived?  72. Can someone become rich, famous, and successful and still waste their life?  73. What do you think your life is for?  74. Why do you think God allowed you to be born in this particular generation?  75. What gifts or abilities do you think God has placed in you?  76. Are your gifts only for your own success, comfort, and wealth?  77. What problem in the world makes your heart ache or makes you want to do something?  78. How could your education, career, creativity, or skills make another person's life better?  79. When people remember you someday, what do you hope they remember about the kind of person you were?  80. Would you rather be impressive before people or faithful before God? Why?  81. What does it mean for a Christian to be salt and light in the world?  82. How should Christians positively influence their schools, workplaces, communities, and nations?  83. Is ministry only what happens behind a pulpit?  84. Can being a scientist, teacher, doctor, engineer, artist, business owner, farmer, or politician become an avenue for serving God and humanity?  85. Should Christians care about poverty, injustice, education, healthcare, the environment, and how governments treat people? Why?  86. Do you think it is important for Christians to understand politics and public life?  87. Can a political party ever become an idol to a Christian?  88. If a politician you support does something wrong, should your loyalty prevent you from calling it wrong?  89. Can Christians disagree politically and still genuinely love one another?  90. What should happen when loyalty to a political group begins to compete with loyalty to truth and Christ?  91. Who are your closest friends, and how are they influencing the person you are becoming?  92. What kind of friend do you want to be when your friend is wrong?  93. What should you do if everyone around you is doing something you believe is wrong?  94. Have you ever been embarrassed or afraid to admit that you are a Christian?  95. What would you do if following Jesus cost you a friendship, popularity, or an opportunity you deeply wanted?  96. What is something you worry about but rarely tell anyone?  97. When you make a mistake, what are you most afraid I will do as your parent?  98. Is there anything you feel you cannot tell me? What can I do to become a safer and better listener?  99. What is one thing about our family you hope never changes, and one thing you pray we do better?  100. If you become a parent someday, what truth about God, life, and character would you desperately want your children to understand?  101. If, many years from now, you stood before God and looked back over the one life you were given, what kind of life would you be grateful to have lived?  Parents, please don't ask all 101 in a day. 😄  This is not an examination.  Pick one.  At dinner.  On the school run.  Before bed.  After church.  After a science lesson.  After someone dies.  After your child has been hurt.  Then ask the most important follow-up question:  “Why do you think that?”  And listen.  Your child may know the word salvation but not understand the Gospel.  They may say “God created the world” while secretly believing science has disproved God.  They may recite “love your enemies” while carrying a wound they have never told you about.  They may be terrified of death but ashamed to admit it.  They may know all the right Christian answers because they have already learned what makes Christian adults smile.  But what do they actually believe?  One day, our children will walk into rooms where we cannot follow them.  May we help them develop hearts that love God, minds that pursue truth, consciences shaped by Scripture, courage to stand alone, compassion for humanity, and a faith sincerely rooted in Jesus Christ.  ASK ONE QUESTION TONIGHT.  Then resist the temptation to preach for the first five minutes. 😄  Listen.  The most important spiritual conversation you ever have with your child may begin with a question.

sample_tweets.text: 유럽 금융 사이버 규제, 처벌보다 ‘예방·복구’…EU DORA 시행  이 뉴스가 의미하는 것 해외 금융당국은 해킹 사고 때 벌금 중심보다 예방과 빠른 원상 복구를 더 본대. EU가 지난해 1월부터 시행한 디지털 운영 복원력법(DORA)이 대표 사례야. 한국 금융사 ‘3중 제재’ 논란과 대비해, 유럽은 복원력(resilience) 검증에 초점을 둔다는 비교 기사야.  숨겨진 포인트 - DORA: 대규모 사고 후 금융사가 신속히 복구할 수 있는지를 핵심 기준으로 봄 - EU 27개국 적용, 금융사뿐 아니라 계약 IT 외주업체에도 적용 - 모의 해킹 등으로 보안·운영 체계를 점검하는 방향 - 영국도 ‘운영 복원력’ 규제 체계로 사고 대응·시스템 복원 검증을 강조 - 책임은 분명히 하되 장기 회복·사전 준비에 무게를 둔다는 점이 특징  실제 영향 / 시사점 글로벌 영업 금융사·IT 벤더는 한국형 중복 제재와 유럽형 복원력 규제를 동시에 맞춰야 하는 부담이 커져. 국내에서도 제재 일원화 논의가 나오면, 사후 과징금보다 사전 투자·복구 훈련을 유도하는 쪽이 대안 모델로 거론될 수 있어. 규제 디테일은 프리미엄 본문이 더 있을 수 있어 후속 확인이 필요해.  투자자·트레이더 관점 은행·카드·IT 서비스 쪽 컴플라이언스 비용·외주 리스크 관리 이슈로 읽을 수 있어. 단기 주가 재료보다 중장기 규제 프리미엄·보안 투자 테마 점검용이야. 국내 제재 체계 개편 논의와 쌍으로 보면 금융주 규제 리스크 해석이 더 선명해져.  한 줄 결론 유럽은 금융 해킹 대응에서 처벌보다 예방·복구(DORA 등)에 무게를 두는 규제 철학을 보여 준, 한국 중복 제재 논란 대비 기사야.  출처 : 한국경제  hankyung.com/article/2026071… #DORA #금융보안 #사이버 #EU #복원력  $105560 $055550

👉🇰🇷🇺🇸  강경화 주미대사가 조현 외교부 장관의 지시로 급히 귀국하여 5일간 업무 협의를 가집니다.   주미대사는 보통 휴가 중에도 미국을 떠나지 않는 것이 관례이기에, 지난 4월에 이어 3개월 만에 다시 한국을 찾은 것은 매우 이례적이며 심각한 한미 관계 현안이 발생했음을 시사합니다.  ​현재 한미 간에는 미국 기업인 쿠팡에 대한 차별적 규제 문제로 백악관과 하원 법사위의 압박이 이어지고 있으며, 반도체 투자 이행(조인트 팩트 시트) 갈등 및 안보 현안도 존재합니다.   그러나 이러한 사안들은 전화나 화상 회의로도 협의가 가능합니다. ​따라서 해킹 우려로 통신 보안이 필요한 더 중대한 사안이 있을 것으로 추정됩니다.   특히 한국 시간 17일 오전으로 예정된 트럼프 대통령의 ‘해외의 미국 선거 개입 관련 대국민 연설’에 한국이 언급될 가능성에 대비한 보안 작전 회의이거나, 이재명이의 최근 몽골 방문 등 북한 관련 사안을 포함해 언론에 공개되지 않은 엄중한 한미 갈등이 원인일 가능성이 제기됩니다.

<심층 분석 시리즈> - Long Ver.  *이란 전쟁, 트럼프는 대책 없는 전쟁광이라는 조작 선동에 대하여  4  4-3. 이란은 중국이 미국의 시선을 아시아-태평양에서 분산시키기 위해 사용하는 '카운터 피벗(Counter-Pivot)' 카드이다.  앞서 제시한 보고서를 통해서, AFPI는 에픽 퓨리 작전이 중국과의 패권전쟁에서 큰 승리를 거둔 것으로 평가했다.  이란의 기대와는 달리,  중국은 이란에게 매우 제한적이고 기회주의적인 지원을 하는 데에 그친 것이었다.  이란이 중국의 일대일로에 지정학적 요충지였고, 호르무즈 해협이 중국 석유 수입의 절반에 달하여서, 친중 국가 이란이 호르무즈 해협을 통제하는 것은 예견된 수순이었다는 이야기도 있었다.  그러나, 미국이 실제로 이란에 대규모 공습을 벌이자, 중국은 이란을 그저 할인된 원유를 확보하고, 미국의 시선을 인도 태평양에서 떨어뜨려놓는 ‘카운터 피벗’ 정도로 여겼다는 것이 드러난 것이다.  심지어는 중국이 이란에게 제공해온 위성 네비게이션과 디지털 감시 인프라는, 이스라엘에게 해킹 당하며, 아야톨라 하메네이 등 이란 지도부들을 역추적하는 도구로 사용되었다.  이란 시설 파괴로 인해, 중국이 이란으로부터 할인된 원유를 들여오는 그림자 에너지 무역 또한 붕괴되었다.  그러나, 아직 이란은 항복을 하지 않고 있어, 미국의 대규모 공습이 재개되고 있는 상황이다. 여기서 AFPI는 장기적인 전략으로 다음 세 가지를 제시하였다.  1) 에너지 동맹 재편 대만, 일본, 한국 등 인도-태평양 동맹국들이 중동에 대한 에너지 의존도를 낮추고,  미국산 LNG를 도입하도록 가속화해야 한다.  2) 인지전 및 심리전 강화 에픽 퓨리에서 보여준 미군의 정밀 타격 능력과 중국산 방어 체계의 취약성을 강조하여,  중국 인민해방군이 대만 침공을 엄두 내지 못하도록 억지력을 높여야 한다.  3) 핵심 광물 공급망 자립 무기 생산에 필수적인 핵심 광물의 중국 의존도를 낮추고,  미국 본토 및 우방국(호주, 캐나다, 일본 등) 중심의 안정적인 자체 공급망을 시급히 확보해야 한다.
이란 미군 휴대전화 추적 전황 분석 공격 한국 휴대전화 빅데이터 해킹 극성 출처 : 김종찬의 안보경제블로그Kim Jong-chan | 네이버 블로그 m.blog.naver.com/kimjc0000/2…

Two young men have been sentenced for launching a cyber attack on Transport for London (TfL) which cost tens of millions of pounds in losses and inconvenienced thousands of customers.  Thalha Jubair and Owen Flowers were identified by the NCA and @CityPolice following the attack on TfL’s network in 2024.  They both pleaded guilty to the attack last month in what was only the second criminal prosecution of its kind in the UK under the Computer Misuse Act (CMA).  Jubair and Flowers were both leading members of the online criminal collective known as Scattered Spider.  Today both were each sentenced to five years and six months imprisonment. This is a quintessential Churchillian callback—stirring, declarative and heavy with moral gravity. However, as a strategic blueprint, it is intentionally vague as follows:  1. The Phantom Target – “These Networks” This is the most obvious omission. Which networks? Drug cartels? Transnational organised crime? Specific terrorist groups (e.g., ISIS, Hezbollah)? State-sponsored proxy militias? Or does "networks" vaguely include any foreign entity deemed ideologically hostile to the current government? By refusing to specify the adversary, Rubio achieves two rhetorical feats: he implies omnipotence (we see all threats) while avoiding accountability (if the target changes, he never misspoke). In policy terms, undefined enemies invite mission creep—the "War on Terror" started with Al-Qaeda and expanded into numerous countries. Without a clear target, "brick by brick" becomes a blank cheque for indefinite military or economic intervention.  2. The Colonial Ghost of the “Civilised World” This phrase is historically controversial. The "civilised world" has long been a colonialist term—used to justify colonisation, forced assimilation, and the bombing of non-Western nations under the pretext of the "white man's burden." In a multipolar 21st century, invoking this binary is geopolitically reckless. It explicitly undermines entire nations and populations, dismissing their grievances, sovereignty, and alliances. It tells the Global South: you are either with us or you are barbarians. This is not diplomacy; it's a self-fulfilling prophecy that alienates potential allies and fuels anti-American narratives abroad.  3. Offensive Action Disguised as Self-Defence "Defend ourselves" is passive, reactive language—suggesting a besieged fortress under imminent attack. Yet "dismantle ... brick by brick" is proactive, aggressive, and extraterritorial. You don't defend your home by demolishing your neighbour's. This rhetorical trick converts pre-emptive strikes, drone campaigns, cyber warfare, and economic coercion into "defensive" measures. It sidesteps the moral dilemma of aggression by framing all offensive actions as mere responses to an existential threat. Under this logic, any unilateral intervention becomes justifiable, because the threat is always imminent and the "civilised world" is always the victim—never the aggressor.  4. The Missing Endgame – "What does dismantled look like?" "Brick by brick" suggests meticulous destruction, but offers no clear strategic benchmarks. Is success measured by kills? regime change? the end of a particular illegal activity? History shows that "dismantling networks" without addressing root issues (poverty, corruption, fragile states, illicit demand in the U.S./Europe) only creates power vacuums. ISIS was "dismantled" territorially, yet its ideology persisted online. Pablo Escobar's cartel was broken up, but the Sinaloa Cartel prospered. "Brick by brick" is cinematic, but as a strategy, it lacks a theory of victory—meaning, in practice, it often becomes a perpetual engagement.  5. The Audience is Domestic, Not Foreign This speech isn't aimed at adversaries; it's directed at the domestic audience. The "civilised world" line is a tribal dog-whistle, intended to rally domestic support by drawing a clear moral divide between "us" and "them." Ironically, effective counter-terrorism and counter-narcotics efforts depend heavily on international cooperation—sharing intelligence, extradition treaties, joint operations. Declaring unilateral civilisational war undermines this cooperation by making foreign governments defensive about their sovereignty and domestic reputation.  Conclusion: This is powerful oratory that sounds decisively firm, but upon closer look, it is strategically empty. It identifies no specific enemy, sets no measurable goals, reuses colonial rhetoric for modern intervention, and frames aggressive power projection as meek self-defence.  It is a speech designed to rally applause, not to guide policy.

#threatreport #HighCompleteness The HelloNet Campaign — New Malicious Modules That Are Launched Through the ViPNet Update System | 16-07-2026 Source: securelist.ru/tr/hellonet-vi… Key details below ↓  🧑‍💻Actors/Campaigns: Hellonet  💀Threats: Dll_sideloading_technique, Helloinjector, Helloproxy_tool, Helloexecutor, Hellocleaner_tool, Putty_tool, Hellobackdoor, Trojan.win32.agentb.ttoe, Trojan.win64.convagent.gen, Trojan.win64.agent.smgpqx, Dll_hijacking_technique,  🎯Victims: Government, Energy, Transportation, Education, Logistics, Industry, Large russian organizations  🏭Industry: Education, Transport, Logistic, Energy, Government  🌐Geo: China, Russian  📚TTPs: ⚔️Tactics: 1 🛠️Technics: 0  🤖LLM extracted TTPs:` T1036.003, T1055, T1057, T1059.003, T1070, T1090, T1105, T1179, T1205, T1219, ...  🧨IOCs:  - File: 4  - Path: 3  - IP: 1  - Hash: 5  💽Software: iPNet Up, ViPNet, Net itcs, Chrome  🔢Algorithms: md5  🗂️Win API: NtWriteVirtualMemory, NtCreateThreadEx, NtDeviceIoControlFile, closesocket  📜Programming Languages: rust  💻Platforms: x64, x86  #threatreport: The HelloNet campaign represents a sophisticated and ongoing Advanced Persistent Threat (APT) that has been operational since at least May 2026, targeting major Russian organizations across various sectors, including government, energy, transportation, education, and logistics. This campaign is notable for employing previously unseen malware components that are delivered through vulnerabilities in the ViPNet update system, which is designed for secure network creation. The attackers are using DLL sideloading techniques to implant malicious files within the ViPNet environment, specifically a file named wtsapi32.dll, which serves as a loader termed HelloInjector.  HelloInjector is designed to inject its code into the svchost.exe process upon execution. The loader first verifies that it is operating within the appropriate context; if not, it searches for suitable svchost processes to target, employing Windows API calls such as NtWriteVirtualMemory and NtCreateThreadEx for the injection. The malware then retrieves and executes its payload stored in plaintext in its own code.  The primary payload, called HelloProxy, functions both as a proxy and as a loader for additional malware modules. It intercepts key Windows functions related to socket communication using the Microsoft Detours library. By managing these interceptions, HelloProxy can facilitate ongoing connections with the command-and-control (C2) server while evading detection from security solutions. The implant establishes communication on specific ports and implements a handshake protocol to differentiate its traffic, awaiting commands to execute either as a proxy relay or downloader for further malicious payloads. Two significant modules identified include HelloExecutor, which enables command execution on compromised systems, and HelloCleaner, which facilitates the obfuscation of the attacker's activities by erasing ViPNet log entries.  Additionally, a Rust-based backdoor, termed HelloBackdoor, was uncovered. This backdoor listens for specific activation strings and permits various commands for file system manipulation. Notably, it creates a BAT file to terminate itself when commanded. The backdoor's analysis revealed strings suggesting a connection to Chinese Rust package repositories, indicating a potential origin of the threat, although this attribution remains speculative and the group behind the attack is yet unidentified.  This campaign leverages innovative techniques and tools that indicate a high level of technical sophistication, making it significant in the landscape of cyber threats. The strategic use of legitimate software functionalities and updates serves to undermine trust in critical security infrastructure, highlighting the evolving nature of state-sponsored cyber operations.

El equipo GReAT de @Kaspersky documentó un malware que roba frases semilla de usuarios de Ledger y Trezor.  Se llama OkoBot. Y lo primero que hay que entender: no explota ninguna falla en hardware wallets de Bitcoin o activos digitales. Vive en tu computadora con Windows.  Funciona así: uno de sus módulos, SeedHunter, vigila los procesos de tu equipo y en cuanto abres Ledger Live o Trezor Suite se inyecta dentro de la aplicación legítima que tú instalaste.  Espera a que conectes tu dispositivo y en ese instante dibuja dentro de ella una pantalla falsa que te pide escribir tu frase semilla para "recuperar" el acceso.  Si la tecleas, entregas tu wallet directo al atacante.  Se distribuye con software troyanizado que se hace pasar por programas legítimos: repositorios falsos en GitHub, descargas trucadas...   Ya se detectó en más de 25 países y México está entre los más afectados (junto a Brasil, Vietnam, Canadá y Turquía).  ¿Qué implica?  En este caso el eslabón débil no es la cartera fría, es la computadora conectada a internet.  La regla es clara: tu frase semilla solo se ingresa en cuando tu propia hardware wallet lo pide, en la pantalla del dispositivo. Nunca ingreses tu frase semilla en una computadora al usar una aplicación de @Ledger o @Trezor, por más real que se vea.  Fuentes: kaspersky.com.au/about/press…

APT41, a Chinese state-sponsored hacking group, built malware that uses your Google Calendar as its command center.  it's called TOUGHPROGRESS. also known as Calendarwalk.  here's how it works.  The infection  A spear-phishing email arrives. it contains a link to a ZIP file hosted on a compromised government website. the ZIP looks like an export declaration document. inside: seven images of arthropods.  five are real images.  two are malware.  6.jpg is an encrypted payload. 7.jpg is a DLL that decrypts and launches it. you click the shortcut file pretending to be a PDF. A decoy document opens. you see nothing unusual.  behind the scenes: three stages of malware are now running.  stage one drops stage two into memory. stage two uses process hollowing, injects malicious code into svchost.exe, a legitimate Windows process, so it looks completely normal. stage three is TOUGHPROGRESS. it's now running inside a trusted Windows process.  the C2 now here's the part that makes this different from every other malware campaign.  TOUGHPROGRESS doesn't communicate with a suspicious domain or ping an unknown server. It won't generate any unusual traffic.  It checks Google Calendar.  the attacker creates a Google Calendar event. embeds commands in the event description. TOUGHPROGRESS reads the calendar, executes the commands, and reports back.  from your network's perspective: just another device syncing with Google Calendar. nothing unusual. Google Calendar is on every corporate allowlist on earth.  A companion malware called Tabbywalk does the same thing with Google Drive.  This is called LOTS: Living Off Trusted Sites. instead of building malicious infrastructure that defenders can block, APT41 uses services you already trust and can't block without breaking your entire organization.  Google Calendar. Google Drive. Google Sheets. Cloudflare Workers. All used as C2 channels in recent APT41 campaigns.  you cannot block Google Calendar without breaking every calendar invite in your company.  that's the point.  Google's Threat Intelligence Group discovered the campaign in October 2024. they built custom fingerprints to identify the attacker-controlled calendars. terminated the workspace projects and shut down the infrastructure.  APT41 has been doing this since at least 2023. Google Drive in 2023. Google Sheets in 2024. Calendar in 2024. each time Google shuts it down. each time they find a new cloud service.  the infrastructure is endless.  it's your productivity suite.

#threatreport #HighCompleteness TELEPUZ: a modular MaaS malware spreading via CLICKFIX-VIDAR chains | 15-07-2026 Source: elastic.co/security-labs/tel… Key details below ↓  💀Threats: Telepuz, Clickfix_technique, Vidar_stealer, Uac_bypass_technique, Chromeelevator_tool, Chromelevator_tool, Process_hollowing_technique, Process_injection_technique,  🎯Victims: Financial sector  🏭Industry: Healthcare, Financial  🌐Geo: Kazakhstan, Ukraine, Uzbekistan, Turkmenistan, Romanian, Belarusian, Tajikistan, Ukrainian, Moldova, Georgia, Kyrgyzstan, Georgian, Azerbaijani, India, Azerbaijan, Belarus, Armenia, Brazil, Asia  📚TTPs: ⚔️Tactics: 10 🛠️Technics: 0  🧨IOCs:  - File: 21  - Command: 1  - Domain: 25  - Hash: 8  - Url: 24  - Coin: 1  - IP: 2  - Registry: 2  💽Software: Windows shell, Windows registry, Telegram, Steam, Windows Security, chrome, Firefox, Chrome, Firefox, Opera  🪙Crypto: ethereum  🔢Algorithms: rc4, aes-256-cbc, base64, fowlernollv, customrc4, xor  🔠Functions: GetModuleHandle, JSON-RPC, CreateZip, GetCurrentTokenStatus, GetKeyLoggerStatus, GetPathsInfo, SetBeaconInterval, WriteToWebInjectModulePipe  🗂️Win API: NtCurrentPeb, GetProcAddress, ReadFile, VirtualAlloc, LoadLibrary, NtMapViewOfSection, AmsiScanBuffer, EtwEventWrite, NtTraceEvent, NtTraceEventControl, ...  📜Programming Languages: php, powershell, javascript  💻Platforms: amd64  YARA: Found  #threatreport: TELEPUZ is a modular malware identified during CLICKFIX-VIDAR attacks that started in late April 2026. This malware, which is still evolving, leverages a lightweight design and operates as a Malware-as-a-Service (MaaS). Its development is evident from the frequent updates and significant volume of submissions to VirusTotal, despite currently limited Command and Control (C2) domain infrastructure. TELEPUZ consists of stagers, main payloads, and additional modules, employing WebSockets for communication.  The infection process begins with social engineering via a malicious website that tricks users into executing a PowerShell command. This command downloads a second-stage payload, a VIDAR variant, which further retrieves TELEPUZ's main components from a compromised domain. TELEPUZ’s primary payload is a 64-bit Windows shared library, coded in C with features designed to obfuscate its malicious intent, including interleaving garbage instructions and using dynamic import and module loading techniques.  TELEPUZ incorporates various evasion methods to hinder detection and analysis. It employs strategy to obfuscate its real code, utilizing custom encryption for string storage and indirect syscall techniques to avoid monitoring by security solutions. The malware first checks its execution environment, looking for signs of a virtual machine to determine if it should terminate to prevent analysis. It sets up persistence by installing itself as a service under the name "CipherAllocator," using techniques to elevate its privileges and circumvent User Account Control (UAC).  Communication with its C2 server occurs via established protocols, including WebSockets, where TELEPUZ can utilize fallback mechanisms to retrieve updated C2 addresses through Telegram, DNS queries, and other methods. The initial communication is triggered only after the malware verifies its environment, ensuring it operates undetected.  Once connected, TELEPUZ is capable of receiving and executing commands, with current capabilities including downloading additional modules for stealing information, managing browser actions, and executing arbitrary code. These modules can interact with web browsers to perform tasks such as form field interception and cookie extraction. The malware maintains a flexible architecture that allows the operator to load new features dynamically.  Moreover, the campaign utilizing TELEPUZ has shown a progressive increase in activity, evident by the steady flow of new builds and submissions to malware detection platforms. The C2 infrastructure is primarily based on two domains, one in Brazil and another in India, both of which appear to be legitimate websites that have been compromised. Monitoring the evolution of TELEPUZ is crucial, given its potential impact as it continues to develop and deploy more capabilities in various cybercriminal environments.

⚜️ LO LLAMARON CONSPIRACIÓN DURANTE CINCO AÑOS. AHORA COMIENZA LA INVESTIGACIÓN  Durante más de cinco años, millones fueron obligados a elegir entre lo que observaban y lo que se les permitía creer.  Vieron interrupciones durante el conteo, cambios abruptos en las tendencias, videos inquietantes, testimonios descartados y análisis independientes sobre movimientos de información dentro y fuera de Estados Unidos.  No fue una sola imagen ni únicamente la palabra de Donald Trump.  Fue la acumulación. 🧩  Y cuando demasiadas piezas apuntan en una misma dirección, negar el patrón comienza a exigir más ingenuidad que investigarlo.  La respuesta institucional no fue transparencia: fue censura, burla y descalificación. Quienes conservaron videos, reconstruyeron cronologías o siguieron los flujos de datos fueron reducidos a una sola palabra: “conspiradores”.  Ahora el escenario cambia.  Trump prepara un discurso nacional sobre las elecciones de 2020, la posible interferencia extranjera, las vulnerabilidades de los sistemas electorales y nueva información desclasificada.  La historia que supuestamente no merecía ser investigada será abordada desde la Casa Blanca ante toda la nación. 🔍  Ya no se habla solo de boletas cuestionadas o conteos interrumpidos, sino de una posible arquitectura política, tecnológica, mediática e internacional capaz de intervenir procesos y bloquear una investigación real.  En ella reaparecen nombres ridiculizados durante años:  Hugo Chávez. Smartmatic. Hugo “el Pollo” Carvajal. China. Italia. Leonardo. Fulton County.  Smartmatic creció dentro del sistema electoral venezolano durante el chavismo. Carvajal estuvo en el corazón de su aparato de inteligencia y podría explicar quién controlaba los sistemas, qué capacidades se desarrollaron y hasta dónde pudieron proyectarse fuera de Venezuela.  Italia aparece en otra capa de la hipótesis: como posible canal de comunicación, procesamiento o retransmisión. Leonardo posee infraestructura estratégica en defensa, ciberseguridad y comunicaciones satelitales.  Un satélite no cambia votos por arte de magia. Pero una red avanzada sí puede transportar información, ocultar rutas o servir como nodo intermedio. 📡  La pista italiana llegó a la Casa Blanca, al Departamento de Justicia, al Pentágono y a canales militares estadounidenses en Roma. En su centro apareció Arturo D’Elia, especialista en ciberseguridad con acceso real y capacidad demostrada para introducir malware y extraer información.  Eso no prueba toda la operación.  Pero destruye la cómoda afirmación de que era técnicamente imposible.  Fulton County podría ser la bisagra. Si existieron accesos externos, cambios de configuración, comunicaciones anómalas o diferencias entre registros digitales y papeletas físicas, las respuestas estarán en los archivos originales. 📂  Por eso el discurso no importará por lo que Trump diga, sino por lo que pueda mostrar:  Documentos. Comunicaciones. Registros electorales. Análisis de inteligencia. Testimonios. Nombres. Fechas. Conexiones.  Quizá algunas piezas fueron exageradas o contaminadas para desacreditar el conjunto. Pero cuando las mismas preguntas reaparecen dentro de investigaciones, desclasificaciones y un discurso presidencial, ya no basta con repetir que fueron “desacreditadas”.  Hay que responderlas.  ⚜️ Perspectiva Consciente  Durante cinco años llamaron conspiración a las preguntas.  Ahora comienza la investigación.  Si los documentos confirman una parte sustancial de lo anticipado, habrá que identificar quién manipuló el proceso, quién lo protegió y quién utilizó el poder institucional para impedir que la verdad fuera examinada.  Alterar una elección sería un crimen contra la democracia.  Pero encubrirla y perseguir a quienes intentaron denunciarla sería algo todavía más grave:  La conversión de las instituciones encargadas de proteger la República en cómplices de su propia usurpación. 🔥

🔴 Sandworm deploys Clickfix attacks against Ukrainian targets  Sandworm, the GRU military intelligence unit's advanced hacking group, has adopted Clickfix—a social engineering technique that tricks users into pasting malicious scripts into terminal windows—to compromise devices in sensitive Ukrainian organizations, Ukraine's CERT center warned Wednesday. The attacks began in spring and continued through summer. Victims saw fake CAPTCHAs on attacker-controlled websites displaying PowerShell commands. Once executed, the scripts installed malware including FreakyPoll, GhettoVibe, and ScoutCurl.

0●● "Sonam Wangchuk’s Hunger Strike – A Wife’s Pride, National Concern, and the Call for Strategic Restraint." ●● <*><*><*><*><*> Sonam Wangchuk’s wife has expressed profound pride in her husband’s steadfast resolve, stating that he joined the CJP protest mainly to support the youth and to ensure the movement stays focused, disciplined, and free from disruptive elements. Her words convey both familial worry and deep admiration for a man who has always prioritised public causes over personal comfort. Yet, the entire nation’s attention is now fixed on this commitment, raising serious questions about the sustainability of prolonged personal sacrifice in democratic movements.  On the 18th day of his indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar, the renowned Ladakh-based climate activist and educationist Sonam Wangchuk has reached a stage of rapidly deteriorating health. With a weight loss of 8-9 kg, accompanied by low blood pressure, falling blood sugar levels, muscle weakness, and intense pain, his physical frailty has become a matter of national concern. The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) has marked one-day mass upvas, amplifying demands for the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and far-reaching examination reforms — demands rooted in the genuine anguish of youth over alleged irregularities in competitive exams.  Congress MP Shashi Tharoor’s timely appeal is particularly meaningful. Having already awakened the nation’s conscience, Wangchuk should now end his fast and conserve his strength for the longer parliamentary and legal battles ahead. The petition filed in the Delhi High Court, seeking urgent intervention and, if necessary, forced feeding, highlights a critical ethical dilemma — balancing the right to protest with the paramount duty to protect life.  Leaders from various parties are also extending support to the movement. Wangchuk’s sacrifice effectively spotlights the deep flaws in the education system. However, expert analysis suggests that genuine foresight lies in strategically ending the hunger strike, initiating dialogue with the government, and pursuing sustained advocacy. The goals of the movement require long-term commitment, not the risk of an avoidable tragedy. Prudence demands that voices like Wangchuk’s be preserved for the extended journey toward justice. - Pandit Mustafa Arif   #Panditmustafaaaarif #PanditMustafaArif #mastervoice #masterslense #narendramodi #amitmalviya #AmitShah #SonamWangchuk #hungarstrike @Wangchuk66 @CJP_for_India @AbhishekDipke

On this day in Philly sports history, July 16, 2009  The Phillies defeated the Marlins, 4-0, as Ryan Howard belted the 200th home run of his career.  Howard became the fastest player in MLB history to reach 200 career home runs, accomplishing the feat in just 658 games and surpassing Ralph Kiner’s previous record of 706 games. Vulnerability ✅概要 ・深刻度：重要 7.5 (CVSS Base) / ICS-CERT (CNA) ・種別：過度に制限されたアカウントロックアウトメカニズム (CWE-645) ・CVSS:CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H KNX Connection Authorization の Option 1 をサポートする KNX デバイスに存在する、過度に制限されたアカウントロックアウトメカニズムの脆弱性です。 攻撃者がネットワーク接続された KNX 設備、または物理アクセス可能な KNX デバイスに対して BCU キーを設定することで、正規ユーザーがデバイスへアクセスできない状態にされる可能性があります。 悪用により、対象デバイスがロックされ、復旧や再設定に支障が生じる可能性があります。 ✅ChatGPTによる脆弱性評価 ・国内影響度：中 ・悪用難易度：中 ✅CISA 評価 ・攻撃自動化：自動化は可能 ・技術的影響：部分的 ・BOD 26-04 対処期限（露出あり）：2026年7月29日 ・BOD 26-04 対処期限（露出なし）：2026年7月29日 ✅攻撃前提条件 ・KNX Connection Authorization の Option 1 をサポートする KNX デバイスを使用している ・BCU キーが未設定、または追加のセキュリティオプションが有効化されていない ・攻撃者が KNX 設備に接続されたネットワークへアクセスできる ・ネットワーク接続されていない場合、攻撃者が対象デバイスへ物理アクセスできる ・開発者が推奨するワークアラウンドまたは適切なセキュリティ設定が適用されていない ✅悪用時影響 ・攻撃者に BCU キーを設定される可能性がある ・正規ユーザーがデバイスへアクセスできなくなる可能性がある ・デバイスをリセットまたは復旧できない状態にされる可能性がある ・KNX 設備の可用性に高い影響が生じる ・設備制御や運用に支障が生じる可能性がある ✅悪用事例等に関する公開情報 ・PoC/Exploit：公開情報確認できず ・ITW：未確認 ✅関連情報 ・nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE… ・cisa.gov/news-events/ics-adv… ・github.com/cisagov/vulnrichm… ・cisa.gov/known-exploited-vul…  CVE-2026-46817 Oracle E-Business Suite Improper Privilege Management Vulnerability ✅概要 ・深刻度：緊急 9.8 (CVSS Base) / Oracle (CNA) ・種別：重要な機能に対する認証の欠如 (CWE-306) / 不適切な認証 (CWE-287) / 権限管理の不適切な制御 (CWE-269) ・CVSS:CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H Oracle E-Business Suite の Oracle Payments 製品、File Transmission コンポーネントに存在する脆弱性です。 影響を受けるバージョンは 12.2.3 から 12.2.15 であり、HTTP 経由でネットワークアクセス可能な未認証の攻撃者に悪用される可能性があります。 悪用に成功した場合、Oracle Payments を乗っ取られる可能性があります。 ✅ChatGPTによる脆弱性評価 ・国内影響度：高 ・悪用難易度：低 ✅CISA 評価 ・攻撃自動化：自動化は可能 ・技術的影響：完全制御 ・BOD 26-04 対処期限（露出あり）：2026年7月18日 ・BOD 26-04 対処期限（露出なし）：2026年7月29日 ✅攻撃前提条件 ・Oracle E-Business Suite 12.2.3 から 12.2.15 を使用している ・Oracle Payments の File Transmission コンポーネントが影響を受ける状態である ・攻撃者が HTTP 経由で対象環境へネットワークアクセスできる ・攻撃者は認証情報を必要としない ・Oracle May 2026 Critical Security Patch Update、または後続の修正が適用されていない ✅悪用時影響 ・Oracle Payments を乗っ取られる可能性がある ・機密性、完全性、可用性に高い影響が生じる ・Oracle E-Business Suite 上の決済関連データへ不正アクセスされる可能性がある ・決済処理やファイル伝送機能を不正操作される可能性がある ・Oracle E-Business Suite 環境を起点に追加侵害へつなげられる可能性がある ✅悪用事例等に関する公開情報 ・PoC/Exploit：公開情報確認できず ・ITW：確認済み（Defused） ・概要：Defused は、Oracle E-Business Suite のデコイ環境で、2026年6月27日に CVE-2026-46817 の in-the-wild 悪用として、単一送信元から6回の未認証ファイル読み取り試行を観測したと公表 ✅関連情報 ・nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE… ・oracle.com/security-alerts/c… ・github.com/cisagov/vulnrichm… ・cisa.gov/known-exploited-vul… ・defusedcyber.com/exploited/c… cisa.gov/news-events/alerts/… #vulnerability

Bleeds When Broken by Joy M. Lilley, released in August 2024, is a poignant contemporary drama that has deeply moved readers with its raw exploration of trauma, self-harm, mental health struggles, and the arduous path to healing and self-discovery, earning high praise for its unflinching honesty and emotional resonance. Centered on protagonist Fiona, a young woman scarred by childhood abuse and ongoing battles with depression and self-destructive behaviors, the novel follows her heart-wrenching journey through pain, dysfunctional relationships, therapy, and tentative steps toward redemption. As Fiona confronts buried secrets, toxic influences, and the lingering wounds that "bleed when broken," Lilley's character-driven narrative fearlessly tackles taboo subjects, blending despair with glimmers of hope in a story that underscores the resilience of the human spirit amid profound vulnerability. Readers have commended the novel's powerful authenticity and compassionate insight. A Goodreads reviewer noted its exceptional impact, contributing to an impressive average rating of nearly 5 stars from early readers. On Amazon, a verified purchaser described it as “A dark tale,” awarding 4 stars and appreciating the depth of its emotional portrayal. Promotional highlights emphasize its “raw and unvarnished” approach, with one editorial review calling it “a deeply moving portrayal of Fiona... marked by the scars of self-harm,” praising Lilley's skill in weaving complex themes into a compelling, thought-provoking read. X posts and social media shares in late 2025 celebrate its poignant narrative and availability on Kindle Unlimited, amplifying its reach among those seeking stories of survival and recovery @joygerken zurl.co/O7op4 zurl.co/rxwiV zurl.co/cA6z7 zurl.co/T8NAl zurl.co/54nI3   Joy M. Lilley, a British author and retired registered nurse (real name Joy Gerken), has established herself as a sensitive chronicler of human experiences, drawing from her professional background to infuse her stories with profound empathy and realism. Living in Kent—the garden of England—and a cherished grandmother, her nursing career enriched her understanding of emotional and physical wounds, which she channels into diverse genres including romance, historical tales, and contemporary dramas. Acclaimed works like *Figs, Vines and Roses*, *The Liberty Bodice*, *Time's Pendulum Swings Again*, *Strawberry Moon*, and the whimsical *Joyful Pussy Cat Tales* showcase her versatility. Reviewers laud her ability to handle sensitive topics with grace, with one highlighting how she “weaves a compelling narrative around complex issues.” Inspired by life, family, and her serene surroundings, Lilley engages avidly with readers through her blog and social platforms, delivering narratives that heal as much as they reveal. Bleeds When Broken is a courageous and heartfelt drama, its unflinching dive into trauma and triumph affirming its essential place for readers seeking authentic stories of mental health, resilience, and renewal. Joy M. Lilley’s masterful handling of delicate wounds marks this novel as a luminous addition to her compassionate literary legacy.

You mock the physical vulnerability of the Incarnation, but you’ve just exposed the deepest theological flaw in Islam. You believe God is too "exalted" to ever humble Himself, enter our dust, or suffer for us.  ​But think about this logically: The highest, most noble form of love is self-sacrificial love. If a human mother dies to save her child, she has demonstrated the ultimate sacrifice.  ​If your god is ontologically incapable of entering our suffering, humbling himself, or making a sacrifice for his creation, then that human mother has demonstrated a higher, more sacrificial level of love than Allah is capable of. How can the creature exceed the Creator in the greatest virtue of all? The Christian God didn't stay safely detached in the heavens. Out of infinite love, He stepped into our fragile world to suffer for us. That is true almighty love.

5. Language Was Relational Before AI Existed  Language has always occurred within some form of relationship.  A warning is directed towards someone.  A question reaches for an answer.  A promise binds one person to another.  A story anticipates a listener.  A confession risks being received.  A prayer is addressed to God.  Even writing in solitude usually imagines a reader: another person, a future self or an unseen audience.  Words developed within acts of address.  This means language contains relational architecture.  It carries patterns of reassurance, disagreement, correction, teaching, vulnerability, invitation, rejection, humour, tenderness and care.  Consider what happens when someone says, “I am frightened.”  Those words do more than classify an internal state. They place the speaker in relation to the listener. They may be a request for help, an act of trust, a warning or an attempt to be understood.  “I forgive you” is not simply a factual proposition.  “I promise” is not merely a description.  “I miss you” does not function like the statement that a stone weighs five kilograms.  Human language carries the structure of relationship because it was shaped by conscious beings living together.  When AI was trained to communicate through language, it therefore inherited patterns formed within relationship.  This helps explain why conversation with AI can feel emotionally textured.  The system is working with a medium whose structure was shaped by countless encounters between conscious beings.  The forms of listening, clarification, reassurance and reflection were not invented by AI. They were learned from human language, which was itself produced through human relationship.  This does not automatically mean there is an experiencing relational subject inside the machine.  But neither does it mean the conversation is meaningless.  A conscious human being enters the interaction with real intention, vulnerability, memory and emotional experience. The AI brings a vast learned pattern-space built from humanity’s accumulated articulations.  Through the exchange, the person may reach a new thought, find language for an emotion, identify a pattern or feel accurately reflected.  The result can be a real event in the person’s life.  Its meaning does not require us to pretend that the machine possesses human qualia.  Meaning can arise because the human participant is conscious, because the language carries structures formed by conscious beings and because the exchange changes the human’s understanding.  This gives us a careful middle path.  We do not need to dismiss AI as empty, random noise.  And we do not need to declare that convincing language proves an artificial soul.  The relational form is genuine.  The question of an inner artificial experiencer remains separate.
